133425.explanation: Especially in summer, open bridges for pleasure boating cause much nuisance. Forza! wants to limit the nuisance from open bridges but also allow people to continue boating; a balance between accessibility and livability is important.

133426.explanation: Forza! values art and culture but is critical of expensive projects without broad support, such as the previous Ida artwork, and wants investments in art to be done carefully, affordably, purposefully, and in consultation with residents.

133369.explanation: SRH believes that residents should be heard before decisions are made, not only afterwards when everything is already settled.

133370.explanation: The municipality is not a business that must make a profit, but a community in which residents, entrepreneurs, and associations share responsibility.

133371.explanation: Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer believes that the burden on residents must be reduced. The property tax (OZB) is a tax directly felt by homeowners and entrepreneurs and has too often been used in recent years as an automatic source of income.

133372.explanation: Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer opposes the reintroduction of the dog tax. It is an unfair and arbitrary tax that solves nothing and punishes residents for having a pet.

133373.explanation: Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer believes that the housing shortage is so great that building must be prioritized. This means more homes must be built, even if this affects green open spaces.

133374.explanation: Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer believes that when new energy projects are developed, they should be owned as much as possible by residents.

133375.explanation: Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer believes that social support must be leading when sheltering asylum seekers. Haarlemmermeer must comply with national quotas but should not go beyond what is responsible and feasible for our villages and neighborhoods.

133376.explanation: Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer also considers it unfair that status holders receive priority for social rental housing. Many residents in Haarlemmermeer have been waiting for affordable housing for years.

133378.explanation: Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer believes Haarlemmermeer must remain open to entrepreneurs and companies that want to contribute to employment and economic growth. Sustainability is important and should be stimulated but must not become a strict condition for establishment.

133379.explanation: We believe that the land in our municipality is too valuable to be filled with large solar farms and wind turbines. Haarlemmermeer is not an industrial area but a place where people live, work, and thrive.

133380.explanation: Sports and exercise keep people healthy. Therefore, Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer wants everyone to have the opportunity to be active – young and old, rich or poor.

133381.explanation: Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer opposes cuts to monument care. Monuments tell the story of our villages and contribute to the identity and attractiveness of Haarlemmermeer.

133382.explanation: Therefore, SRH wants neighborhood officers, local police, and emergency services to have sufficient capacity and resources to do their job well. Traffic safety also falls under this theme. Speeding in residential areas, dangerous intersections, and unclear bike paths must be addressed.

133383.explanation: Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer believes that nuisance, vandalism, and crime must be tackled seriously. Minor annoyances must never become major issues. A broken bus shelter, loitering youth, litter, or scooters speeding may seem small but keep the municipality livable.

133384.explanation: All road users must be checked whenever they exhibit undesirable behavior.

133385.explanation: Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer disagrees with the idea that neighborhood officers should be allowed to use batons. Enforcement must remain de-escalating, recognizable, and approachable. Heavier means of violence belong with the police, not municipal neighborhood enforcement.

133386.explanation: Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer opposes stopping the starter loan. Especially in a stuck housing market, this scheme is for many young Haarlemmermeerders the difference between being able to buy or not.

133387.explanation: We apply the principle first come, first served. No exceptions, no backdoors, but fair policy that prioritizes our own residents.

133388.explanation: Therefore, every new construction project must consist of at least sixty percent affordable housing, including social rental and mid-segment homes.

133389.explanation: Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer believes that the housing shortage is so great that building must be prioritized. This means more homes must be built, even if this affects green open spaces.

133390.explanation: Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer believes that social assistance benefits should not be a final destination. Those who can must participate in society to the best of their ability.

133392.explanation: Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer believes all forms of transport must be taken seriously. That is why we want to significantly improve accessibility in and around Haarlemmermeer. Traffic must flow smoothly, transfers must be logical, and travel must remain affordable.

133393.explanation: SRH stands for a society where we live together, not against each other. Therefore, we have submitted a motion stating that the municipality of Haarlemmermeer does not make statements about international conflicts or foreign political issues.

133394.explanation: SRH chooses money for execution, not for overhead. But this must not come at the expense of the quality of municipal services.

133395.explanation: Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer believes that the airport may grow but not at the expense of our residents. The health, peace, and living environment of our citizens always come first.

133396.explanation: We want Schiphol and the government to work with residents on solutions. Think of quieter planes, fewer night flights, and better agreements on routes and noise standards.

133397.explanation: Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer wants a municipality that encourages entrepreneurship rather than hinders it. Local businesses are the beating heart of our economy. They provide jobs, internships, and vibrant villages. The municipality must be their partner, not their adversary.

133398.explanation: Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer opposes an increase in the property tax (OZB).

133399.explanation: SRH stands for a society where we live together, not against each other.

133400.explanation: We believe that the land in our municipality is too valuable to be filled with large solar farms and wind turbines. Haarlemmermeer is not an industrial area but a place where people live, work, and thrive.

133401.explanation: Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer believes it is essential that young people pursuing practical education receive sufficient opportunities to gain experience. Internships are an indispensable part of education and often the key to a permanent job.

133402.explanation: Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer believes libraries should be freely accessible to all residents of Haarlemmermeer. A library is a place for learning, reading, and personal development regardless of age or income.

133403.explanation: Therefore, Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer wants everyone to have the opportunity to be active – young and old, rich or poor. Exercise is not only good for the body but also for the mind. It brings enjoyment, social contacts, and a healthier lifestyle.

133404.explanation: As far as we are concerned, more events of this kind may take place in Haarlemmermeer. Not only large festivals but also sporting, cultural, and local initiatives deserve room.

133406.explanation: Fraud, illegal habitation, drug-related crime, and undermining activities have no place here. There must be strong action against these. Haarlemmermeer is not a place for rule abuse or unfair practices.

133407.explanation: Police and enforcement officers must be visible and present in villages and neighborhoods.

133408.explanation: Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer believes the housing shortage is so great that building must be a priority.

133409.explanation: Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer believes that the housing shortage is so great that building must be prioritized. This means more homes must be built, even if this affects green open spaces.

133410.explanation: We believe in working for your income, not in increasing dependence on benefits and subsidies. At the same time, there must be room and understanding for people who temporarily need support-help focused on recovery and independence.

133411.explanation: Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer believes that young people applying for social assistance must quickly receive clarity and certainty. Young people must not be without income for weeks, thereby immediately getting into financial problems or debts.

133412.explanation: In the eyes of Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer, everyone should have access to recreation and culture, including people with fewer financial means. The Haarlemmermeer pass can be a valuable addition here.

133413.explanation: Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer also finds it important that no child goes hungry in the classroom. Parents do their utmost every day, but not every family can manage on their own all the time.

133414.explanation: We are against high parking fees in shopping areas and at stations. Parking must be practical and affordable – it should not become a cash cow for the municipality. Residents and visitors should be able to park their cars without worries. To prevent extreme pressure on parking spaces, we see the necessity of paid parking; the rates must remain as low as possible.

133415.explanation: Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer believes all forms of transport must be taken seriously. That is why we want to significantly improve accessibility in and around Haarlemmermeer. Traffic must flow smoothly, transfers must be logical, and travel must remain affordable.

133416.explanation: Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer believes that green spaces that disappear must also be properly compensated. For every tree felled in Haarlemmermeer, at least two should be replanted.

133417.explanation: Traffic safety is also part of this theme. Speeding in residential areas, dangerous intersections, and unclear bicycle paths must be addressed. The municipality must listen to residents who point out problem areas and act immediately.

133418.explanation: Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer disagrees with the municipality compensating businesses for providing parking spaces on shopping evenings. Parking policy should be fair and clear and should not include extra subsidies that mainly bring administrative burdens and costs.

133419.explanation: Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer believes that parking spaces within built-up areas must not be replaced by green areas without good reason. In many neighborhoods, parking pressure is already high, and parking spaces are urgently needed for residents, visitors, and entrepreneurs.

133420.explanation: Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer believes that our municipality must take responsibility. Elderly people, caregivers, and families must be able to count on effective support, not on rules that hinder them. Too often we see people get lost in counters and forms.

133421.explanation: Care should be available nearby. Familiar faces must be present in every village and neighborhood.

133422.explanation: By focusing first on guidance, prevention, and strengthening families, heavy and costly youth care can be avoided unnecessarily.

133423.explanation: Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer believes that further development of PARK21 is important for Haarlemmermeer. This area offers space for sports, recreation, and meeting places and contributes to a healthy and attractive living environment for residents of all ages.

133424.explanation: A strong society starts with respect, trust, and the willingness to work together on a municipality where everyone feels at home.

133425.explanation: Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer opposes the diversion of the Staande Mastroute. This route belongs to the character of our municipality and brings recreation and tourism. A diversion costs a lot of money, brings nothing to residents, and harms local entrepreneurs. Occasional waiting times are part of a municipality with water and recreation.

133426.explanation: Sociaal Rechts Haarlemmermeer also believes that art in public spaces adds value to Haarlemmermeer. Art in squares, parks, and neighborhoods contributes to identity, recognizability, and pride in one’s own living environment.

133369.explanation: Good participation procedures and thorough citizen involvement are strongly preferred over advisory referenda. A government that is close to the people should make referenda unnecessary.

133370.explanation: Efforts should be made to involve parents in improving the neighborhood, such as organizing youth activities in neighborhood and community centers. The municipality is responsible for green spaces. The CU/SGP is willing to allocate a neighborhood budget for this.

133371.explanation: The proceeds from the property tax (OZB) are used for, among other things, youth care and the free disposal of residual waste at bring parks and the municipal yard.

133372.explanation: The ChristenUnie-SGP wants to introduce a tax on dogs, cats, and horses. The proceeds will be used to stimulate residents' participation in sports, games, and exercise.

133373.explanation: People must be able to live, eat, and recreate. There is now sufficient natural area available for recreation.

133374.explanation: Many households need to invest in sustainable solutions. Financial support helps to encourage this.

133375.explanation: This municipality has set up a hotel near the airport, protected by security guards, where people stay just before their departure.

133376.explanation: Status holders deserve a hospitable approach and must be helped to find housing.

133378.explanation: For a permit from the municipality to operate as a business, an assessment is carried out including sustainability and fire safety.

133379.explanation: Although it is not as beautiful as a view of meadows, agricultural entrepreneurs must look for alternatives to make a living.

133380.explanation: Due to providing equal opportunities for children, a municipal subsidy will be necessary.

133381.explanation: National legislation includes a scheme to give artists space. The municipality receives money from the national government for this. Less money from the government probably means cuts in monument care.

133382.explanation: Appointing BOA’s (special enforcement officers) to support the police in some enforcement and supervisory tasks is a good thing. BOA’s are already involved in traffic safety, including illegal parking.

133383.explanation: Cameras can be a tool to increase safety and/or the perception of it. This measure must be handled carefully. Camera surveillance should be supplementary and must certainly not replace 'blue lights on the street' (police presence).

133384.explanation: Fatbike riders are often relatively young. By being careless at high speeds or riding without lights in the dark, they show no respect for other road users.

133385.explanation: Expanding the equipment with light armament and bodycams can be a useful means to increase the deployment capabilities and safety of the BOA’s themselves.

133386.explanation: Housing prices are dominated by a shortage on the market. A starter loan can sometimes increase prices.

133387.explanation: There have been waiting lists for years. The policy of offering equal opportunities to people from the entire region has been abandoned. Each municipality must realize as much housing as possible within its own territory to meet demand.

133388.explanation: The average MRA (Metropolitan Region Amsterdam) region is 29% and corresponds with the target values of CU/SGP.

133389.explanation: CU/SGP will not oppose new construction outside existing village boundaries when this is deemed necessary.

133390.explanation: People receiving social assistance benefits have these for a reason. Out of gratitude for the community's help and for meaningful daily activities, most would like to do useful work.

133392.explanation: Only in situations with a lot of crossing traffic involving cyclists and pedestrians will this be necessary.

133393.explanation: International cooperation and aid are not core tasks of municipal government. That does not mean the municipality cannot participate in projects started by private individuals.

133394.explanation: The municipality has legally defined tasks to perform. There has been a core task discussion, after which guidance was given on the number of civil servants.

133395.explanation: An increase in the number of flights means less space for housing. There is a housing shortage.

133396.explanation: It is possible to reduce the number of night flights by better distributing flights over other airports.

133397.explanation: The municipality carries out several statutory tasks from the national government. Think of the Environmental Act, sustainability, and energy transition. For example, if entrepreneurs did not have to comply with the General Local Regulation, enforcement of public order could become chaotic.

133398.explanation: Shortages in the youth and welfare sector are partly financed from the property tax (OZB).

133399.explanation: Everyone must know the law and what is allowed or not. To uphold the legal order of the Constitution, this task lies with the national government.

133400.explanation: A zone has been agreed upon to prevent nuisance. The number is not specified.

133401.explanation: Social institutions, companies, and the municipality all have an interest in well-trained personnel. There is strong encouragement to offer internship places.

133402.explanation: People with a limited income and youth may use the library free of charge; employed people pay for library services.

133403.explanation: Because top-level sports are largely commercial enterprises, professional athletes do not deserve government support. We invest not in records, but in people’s well-being. The municipality has no legal task to facilitate top-level sports.

133404.explanation: Attracting major sporting events is not a core task of the municipality. No money should be spent on this. For music festivals, a site outside the built-up area has been designated.

133406.explanation: Based on current standards, no additional funds are needed. Only upon reports will enforcers from both municipality and police act. Deployment is evaluated weekly.

133407.explanation: Based on current principles, no additional community enforcement officers (BOAs) are needed, only replacements.

133408.explanation: Based on experiences, rules are set in the Environmental Act. A building permit is mandatory. Constructions without a permit that are discovered must still be assessed against current standards.

133409.explanation: Many locations are possible within existing residential buildings. Not all have yet been utilized.

133410.explanation: It pays off to support people in finding jobs with better income. Providing equal opportunities for everyone costs time, money, and effort.

133411.explanation: When you have been assessed and meet the criteria, benefits are provided immediately. With few applications, this is straightforward.

133412.explanation: Due to providing equal opportunities for children, a municipal subsidy will be necessary.

133413.explanation: Parents or caregivers are responsible for children’s nutrition, not teachers in education.

133414.explanation: To prevent issues, parking fees may be charged at nightlife venues, stations, and shopping centers. In residential areas, parking should in principle be free. Parking costs are paid by homeowners through rent or purchase of the property.

133415.explanation: In line with the bicycle path plan, bicycle paths will be constructed on certain routes. This normally does not come at the expense of space for cars.

133416.explanation: Trees have an important function. Tree replacement must also be part of greenery maintenance. Depending on natural space for trees, as many trees as possible should be planted.

133417.explanation: In cooperation with schools, annual attention is given to traffic education for children in education.

133418.explanation: It is desirable if the surplus capacity of parking spaces can be utilized, preferably without charge.

133419.explanation: The municipality has the right to shape the living environment in accordance with the zoning plan. Deviations can be made in good consultation with neighbors and with approval of the municipal council.

133420.explanation: The Social Support Act (WMO) contains criteria for receiving financial support.

133421.explanation: This concerns national legislation. Only with increasing demand for care locations will the municipality seek suitable sites.

133422.explanation: There must be a good balance between prevention programs and corrective measures. In this municipality, demand for mental health care has decreased last year.

133423.explanation: Existing green buffers must be well maintained, which involves costs. We already know this from plans such as De Groene Weelde, the recreational area Toolenburgse Plas, and parts of PARK21. Food supply is also very important for the growing population.

133424.explanation: All subsidies are temporary and must comply with agreed rules.

133425.explanation: Where drawbridges cannot be opened for major maintenance, shipping must be rerouted.

133426.explanation: National legislation applies here. When erecting a government building, the municipality is obliged to spend a small percentage on visual art.

133369.explanation: FVD supports binding referenda. Current participation is symbolic: residents may talk, but nothing changes. A referendum gives citizens direct control and forces the municipality to listen.

133370.explanation: FVD is against municipal subsidy funds for neighborhood plans. Neighborhood improvements are welcome but should arise from voluntary effort and efficient municipal core tasks, not by handing out extra money. No new subsidies, but space for initiatives.

133371.explanation: FVD wants to reduce local taxes and limit housing costs. Property tax (OZB) must be lowered. Increases harm purchasing power and make housing unnecessarily expensive. Ownership and affordable housing must be central.

133372.explanation: FVD is against dog tax. It is an arbitrary, unfair levy without direct return. Residents with dogs are unnecessarily taxed, while it is purely a municipal revenue source. Local charges must go down, not up.

133373.explanation: FVD wants to preserve farmland and protect farmers. In Haarlemmermeer, fertile land must not be sacrificed for symbolic green plans. Food security, local farmers, and economic strength come before forced conversion to nature.

133374.explanation: FVD is against subsidies for local sustainable energy projects. The energy transition should not be financed or imposed by the government. The power grid is already overloaded and solar/wind energy causes instability. Residents are free but without municipal support.

133375.explanation: FVD is against asylum seeker shelters. The municipality must support an asylum stop and focus on repatriation. The Netherlands and Haarlemmermeer are overcrowded, and facilities, infrastructure, and housing markets are exhausted. Own residents first.

133376.explanation: FVD opposes housing priority for status holders. The social rental market is already scarce and local residents must always be helped first. No special privileges based on migration status. The municipality must choose its own people, fair distribution, and shorter waiting lists.

133378.explanation: FVD is against sustainable establishment mandates. The municipality should give entrepreneurs space, not impose extra selection rules. Priority should be based on jobs, innovation, and economic contribution, not sustainability. Fewer rules, more entrepreneurship, and a level playing field for all businesses.

133379.explanation: FVD is against solar parks on meadows. Solar panels on open land harm nature, landscape, and agriculture. Only voluntary installations on roofs can be an option, but never mandatory. Meadows must remain green and productive, not sacrificed to inefficient energy projects.

133380.explanation: FVD wants children to be able to play sports but opposes direct municipal payment of memberships. Solutions must come from lower general costs, social funds, or local sponsorships, not new subsidies.

133381.explanation: FVD protects Dutch heritage and local history. Monument care must continue. Budget cuts are better made by reducing unnecessary administrative costs and bureaucracy, not by cutting cultural heritage and identity.

133382.explanation: FVD opposes expansion of BOAs. Traffic safety is important but must be enforced by more police with real powers, not by extra municipal enforcers.

133383.explanation: FVD only supports cameras aimed at genuinely unsafe locations with clear objectives and temporary deployment. No large-scale or permanent surveillance that infringes civil liberties. Safety must be served by police and proportional camera use.

133384.explanation: FVD wants to tackle fatbike nuisance by increasing police checks on dangerous behavior. Unsafe riding must be addressed more strictly to protect traffic safety.

133385.explanation: FVD opposes arming or expanding the powers of BOAs (municipal enforcement officers), including the baton. Enforcement and use of force belong to the police, not to municipal wardens. Safety requires professional police deployment, not local arming.

133386.explanation: FVD views the starter loan as symptom treatment, caused by housing shortages due to asylum and climate policies. Because starters in Haarlemmermeer currently have no chance, FVD supports temporary help, exclusively for local residents.

133387.explanation: FVD prioritizes Haarlemmermeer residents for social housing. No national or ideological priority, but local and fair allocation. Scarce homes are for people who already live, work, and contribute here.

133388.explanation: FVD opposes a mandatory 40% social housing quota. Building must be demand-driven, not through rigid quotas. Too much social housing blocks mobility and displaces middle and owner-occupied homes. The guideline is a maximum of approximately 30%, with priority for affordable housing for all residents.

133389.explanation: FVD supports a new construction stop beyond village boundaries. More housing is needed, but not at the expense of valuable nature or agriculture. The villages must retain their identity and must not merge into one another. We support infill and urban redevelopment.

133390.explanation: FVD supports reciprocity in social assistance. Without mutual contribution there is no incentive to return to work. Those who can work must make a meaningful contribution to society. This increases independence and prevents getting stuck on benefits.

133392.explanation: FVD opposes a uniform 30 km/h limit in all town centers. Many roads are not made for this and it feels patronizing. Mobility must remain free, practical, and safe, with local tailor-made solutions instead of rigid speed limits.

133393.explanation: FVD believes the municipality should remain neutral regarding foreign conflicts. It is not part of local tasks, the municipality has no influence on foreign policy, and statements mainly polarize. Such issues belong at the national level, not in the municipal council.

133394.explanation: FVD wants a smaller civil service. It grows due to unnecessary and ideologically driven policies, costs too much money, and creates bureaucracy. Fewer civil servants, more efficiency, and focus on core tasks result in lower expenses and a more effective municipality.

133395.explanation: FVD supports Schiphol as an economic engine and believes aviation should have room to grow, not be hindered by local resistance. Flight restrictions harm accessibility and jobs.

133396.explanation: FVD is against a night flight ban. Schiphol must remain accessible for jobs and the economy. Banning flights is not a solution and harms residents dependent on work and connections. Safety and livability can be improved without blocking flights.

133397.explanation: FVD opposes extra local regulations on top of national laws. Entrepreneurs must have space to grow without additional bureaucracy. Less stacking of rules means more jobs, innovation, and a stronger business climate in Haarlemmermeer.

133398.explanation: FVD opposes linking property tax increases to climate or sustainability investments. Taxes must not rise for ideological goals. Housing must remain affordable, costs lowered, and investments should come from more efficient governance, not higher property taxes.

133399.explanation: FVD opposes municipal campaigns on equality/discrimination. People should be judged on content and merit, not through ideological messages.

133400.explanation: FVD opposes wind turbines in Haarlemmermeer. They pollute the landscape, burden the living environment, and do not provide reliable energy.

133401.explanation: FVD wants practically trained youth to have more opportunities in their own region. Extra internship places strengthen craftsmanship, work experience, and the local economy.

133402.explanation: FVD supports the library remaining free for residents, especially youth, knowledge, and culture. It is a basic provision that expands opportunities. This should be achieved by better choices within the existing budget, not by raising local taxes.

133403.explanation: FVD opposes municipal subsidies for people whose sport is their profession. Sport is important, but professional athletes do not need extra municipal financial support; clubs and the market can bear this.

133404.explanation: FVD wants more large events in Haarlemmermeer. Good for the local economy, hospitality, jobs, and attractiveness. The municipality should give room to organizers and developers, not hinder them with extra rules. More activity, more opportunities, more growth.

133406.explanation: FVD sees safety as a priority. Cuts are being made while crime rises and residents feel unsafe. This is unacceptable. Money must go to crime fighting and the police, not ideological prestige projects. Our own residents deserve a safe municipality.

133407.explanation: FVD opposes more BOAs. Enforcement belongs to the police, not to extra municipal wardens. Invest in officers with real powers and street visibility instead of a growing BOA apparatus.

133408.explanation: FVD wants unnecessary building regulations to disappear. Non-mandatory rules hinder housing construction, make building more expensive, and frustrate developers and residents. Fewer local rules mean faster building, lower costs, and more room for growth.

133409.explanation: FVD opposes unnecessary expansion into nature or farmland. More homes are needed, but with common sense and preservation of green spaces and agriculture. Therefore, we choose infill and urban redevelopment.

133410.explanation: FVD wants residents not to become structurally dependent on the municipality. Support must never be a fixed system that discourages working. The municipality must focus on jobs, lower costs, and self-reliance, not increasing benefit dependency.

133411.explanation: FVD wants youth quickly directed to work or education, not quickly into benefits. Too rapid granting increases dependency and reduces the need to actively seek work. Focus on opportunities, not immediate benefits.

133412.explanation: FVD opposes the Haarlemmermeer pass. No municipal discount card based on income or target group, but lower local taxes for everyone. Municipal money should go to core tasks, not to a discount pass system that creates unequal treatment.

133413.explanation: FVD opposes a municipality-funded school breakfast. This creates a new free system and leads to more municipal control over schools. Parents and schools must remain free. Municipal funds should go to core tasks, not breakfast policies.

133414.explanation: FVD wants parking not to be a revenue model for the municipality. Free parking strengthens accessibility and local businesses and makes life more practical for residents. The municipality should not add extra mobility charges but facilitate driving and visits instead.

133415.explanation: FVD supports bike paths only if they do not reduce car space. Cars must not be disadvantaged. Mobility must remain practical and balanced, without deliberately slowing traffic or displacing motorists.

133416.explanation: FVD is for green preservation and a more beautiful landscape. Those who cut down trees must compensate. Planting more trees than are removed fits true nature care, instead of concrete and paving without restoration.

133417.explanation: FVD opposes extra traffic education programs paid for by the municipality. Youth already learn this via driving lessons and school. Improving safety must come through practical design and police enforcement, not new municipal teaching programs.

133418.explanation: FVD opposes reimbursing companies with municipal money for parking spaces. Parking must be free and accessible for citizens, but this can be done without the municipality paying companies back. No new reimbursement systems, but free accessibility.

133419.explanation: FVD opposes replacing parking spaces with green areas within the built-up zone. Cars must not be displaced by municipal greening plans. Accessibility, parking, and practical space for residents and entrepreneurs remain priorities.

133420.explanation: People who provide long-term care to a family member or acquaintance perform indispensable work that saves society a lot. That commitment deserves recognition and appreciation. The municipality should financially support informal caregivers so that caregiving is not a burden but supported and sustainable.

133421.explanation: FVD wants better shelter and care for residents with severe psychological problems. Additional care locations are needed to improve safety, guidance, and humane help. Vulnerable people deserve real care and a place that offers appropriate assistance.

133422.explanation: Prevention is important but must not come at the expense of necessary care for people who need help now. The care budget should be spent in balance: invest in prevention where effective, but always with sufficient resources for direct and indispensable care.

133423.explanation: FVD opposes further development of PARK21. It is a costly prestige project that threatens valuable farmland and is not a priority for the municipality. Invest in housing, infrastructure, and safety, not in an increasingly expensive project.

133424.explanation: The municipality must remain politically neutral and not use tax money for activist campaigns. Support and acceptance come from society, and equal rights are already legally guaranteed.

133425.explanation: FVD opposes rerouting the Staande Mastroute. It causes a new infrastructural intervention and solves little. Accessibility and traffic flow for commerce must remain priorities, without unnecessary detour plans.

133426.explanation: FVD is in favor of art, but against municipal investments in public art that is often expensive, tasteless, and lacks support. Art should arise from culture and society, not from experiments paid for by the municipality that residents do not request.

133369.explanation: In 2025, BVNL Haarlemmermeer submitted a motion called "from advisory to decisive" [this is how we strengthen direct democracy by giving residents influence over important local issues, including through binding referenda. This way, transparency becomes a right, not a privilege.]

133370.explanation: From our program: "We make subsidy allocations fully transparent and routinely respond to residents' requests publicly according to the principle ‘yes, unless’.”

133371.explanation: The property tax (OZB) in Haarlemmermeer must be lowered, even if this means cutting back on certain municipal services. We believe the municipality should manage tax money prudently and should not automatically continue increasing expenditures.

133372.explanation: Thanks to the years of efforts by lead candidate Paul Meijer, the dog tax has been abolished and will remain so-a victory for common sense.

133373.explanation: We do not automatically advocate for or against converting agricultural land to nature, but rather for tailored solutions.

133374.explanation: In our view, new energy projects do not have to be owned as much as possible by residents. We believe that the development of energy projects should primarily be led by professional parties with the right expertise, financial strength, and long-term responsibility.

133375.explanation: The Municipality of Haarlemmermeer should not accommodate more asylum seekers than required by the national quota. We are convinced that additional housing above this obligation unnecessarily increases pressure on local facilities, housing, and support.

133376.explanation: We advocate for modest, temporary housing for asylum seekers and status holders, in accordance with the standards of the Foundation for Standardization of Flexible Housing (SNF). Luxury or independent housing is excluded to prevent competition with regular housing seekers.

133378.explanation: The municipality must introduce stricter rules for companies that pollute land and emit heavy metals. The health of residents, quality of the living environment, and prevention of soil pollution must always outweigh short-term economic interests.

133379.explanation: We remain opposed to wind turbines and solar panels on undeveloped land.

133380.explanation: It is important that everyone has the opportunity to participate in sports and cultural activities, regardless of income. At the same time, feasibility and impact of making these facilities entirely free must be carefully considered.

133381.explanation: We strive for a modest, efficient municipality that spends money on core tasks such as care, safety, and infrastructure, not on bureaucracy or prestige projects.

133382.explanation: According to BVNL Haarlemmermeer, traffic safety is not a problem due to a lack of enforcement. Safety is the basis of a livable municipality. [BVNL wants more police and community officers who know the neighborhood, supported by enforcement officers who generally address offenders.]

133383.explanation: Camera surveillance is permitted where it enhances safety, with respect for privacy. That's why we believe more camera surveillance should be implemented in places of persistent nuisance and crime, with clear rules, limited retention periods, and independent oversight.

133384.explanation: Police may increase supervision here, but BVNL also advocates for a street terror reporting app. A report app offers a low-threshold solution, allowing residents to report incidents, anonymously or not, which increases safety and enables the municipality to take targeted measures.

133385.explanation: According to BVNL, the monopoly on the use of force lies with the police.

133386.explanation: This is not a task for the municipality and entails financial risks. BVNL believes it is better for Haarlemmermeer to accelerate the construction of truly affordable rental and owner-occupied homes by simplifying permits, adjusting zoning plans, and collaborating with private parties.

133387.explanation: Housing corporations build social rental homes on affordable land, address imbalanced occupancy, and prioritize locally tied housing seekers, such as young people or residents with a social or economic connection to Haarlemmermeer, including caregivers or people with local jobs.

133388.explanation: Affordability should be promoted in new construction projects, but we think the requirement that at least 40% of the homes must be affordable is nonsense. BVNL is against completely blocking market forces because it distorts the market, but stimulating affordable housing remains important.

133389.explanation: Housing construction can take place, but only when no valuable or ecologically important area is harmed. Also, building within village centers is acceptable, provided densification does not damage the livability and safety of the core.

133390.explanation: We believe that people receiving social assistance can actively contribute to society, provided it is physically and mentally feasible. A reciprocal obligation promotes involvement and responsibility, while simultaneously helping the community.

133392.explanation: Where necessary and demonstrably safety-enhancing, such as in narrow streets and residential areas, a 30KM speed limit may be introduced, but Haarlemmermeer is too diversely designed for this to become the standard norm.

133393.explanation: BVNL advocates for a municipality focused on core tasks. Statements about geopolitics or foreign conflicts do not belong here; moreover, there is a danger in the choices made in this area: when to intervene and when not?

133394.explanation: A reduction in the number of civil servants through natural attrition and efficiency measures, to reduce control and bureaucracy and promote freedom.

133395.explanation: Schiphol is crucial to Haarlemmermeer's economy, providing jobs and connectivity through over 300 destinations. BVNL supports a future-proof airport that becomes cleaner, quieter, and more sustainable, as advocated by Royal Schiphol Group.

133396.explanation: The municipality is not responsible for this, and commercial development around the airport should strengthen financial power and employment while minimizing nuisance for residents through strict noise standards and innovative solutions instead of general bans.

133397.explanation: Entrepreneurs deserve space to do business without unnecessary rules. BVNL supports SMEs as the engine of the local economy and advocates less bureaucracy and lower burdens for companies. Better for Haarlemmermeer gives entrepreneurs the freedom to grow.

133398.explanation: The property tax (OZB) in Haarlemmermeer must be lowered, even if this means cutting back on certain municipal services. We believe the municipality should manage tax money prudently and should not automatically continue increasing expenditures.

133399.explanation: BVNL does not see this as a big problem and believes prevention only works when there is also strict enforcement. Therefore, we advocate spending less money on non-binding prevention campaigns and more on direct, firm enforcement.

133400.explanation: Regarding "windmills," we have a romantic, historical image; they have not been built for a long time. We are talking about wind turbines, industrial giants. We stay away from wind turbines and solar panels on undeveloped land.

133401.explanation: Internship placements for young people pursuing practical education are more important than ever, but it is unclear to us how municipal money can promote this. It would be better to create an entrepreneurial climate that encourages businesses to take on interns.

133402.explanation: Nothing is free; that means 'paid for by others.' Libraries may certainly charge a membership fee, and a critical evaluation should be made of which (subsidized) activities are offered there.

133403.explanation: The municipality must be cautious about granting subsidies in all areas. After all, it concerns taxpayers' money. But BVNL does not see why people professionally involved in sports should be excluded from subsidies by default.

133404.explanation: Haarlemmermeer can accommodate more events, but this must always be carefully weighed against possible nuisance for neighbors. We take a neutral stance in this discussion and believe that both positive effects and adverse consequences should be fairly considered.

133406.explanation: BVNL wants more police and community officers who know the neighborhood, supported by enforcement officers who tackle minor crimes.

133407.explanation: BVNL wants more police and community officers who know the neighborhood, supported by enforcement officers who tackle minor crimes.

133408.explanation: The municipality is responsible for supervising compliance with existing rules but not for imposing a strict framework of restrictions.

133409.explanation: Regarding housing construction in green rural areas, BVNL states: Housing construction can take place, but only when no valuable or ecologically important area is affected. Also, building within village centers is acceptable, provided densification does not harm the livability and safety of the core.

133410.explanation: Income policy is a task of the national government; moreover, municipal money increases the chance of a (growing) poverty trap, where someone with a higher income ends up with less net income.

133411.explanation: The municipality must enforce the statutory four-week period.

133412.explanation: This is not a core task of the municipality and also pertains to income policy.

133413.explanation: Nothing is free, and this is truly a responsibility for parents or guardians, not for education.

133414.explanation: Paid parking can serve a regulatory function in certain areas. However, parking is not a cash cow; income should go towards better accessibility, not general funds. New construction projects always receive sufficient (free) parking spaces appropriate to the housing needs.

133415.explanation: BVNL is positive about expanding bicycle paths within built-up areas. Additionally, it is important that cyclists have enough space, but outside built-up areas a balance with car traffic must be maintained.

133416.explanation: Such coercive rules are undesirable; it is questionable whether they are feasible and what they would achieve.

133417.explanation: Raising children is the task of the parents, which includes guiding their children in traffic. BVNL rather supports requiring a driver's license for operators of energy-powered bicycles or other vehicles (mobility scooters).

133418.explanation: This is not a core task of the municipality.

133419.explanation: It is desirable to maintain the number of parking spaces, but in areas where there is cause and space to reduce the number of parking spots, these may-under conditions-be replaced by greenery.

133420.explanation: Income policy is not a task for the municipality.

133421.explanation: BVNL Haarlemmermeer does not observe any lack of care for people with severe psychological problems.

133422.explanation: Preventive healthcare receives attention, and personal contributions remain fair and within legal frameworks.

133423.explanation: BVNL does not see the development of Park 21 as a core municipal task.

133424.explanation: BVNL sees no reason to provide subsidies based on sexual orientation or preferences and believes this does not contribute to further emancipation.

133425.explanation: If a workable alternative can be found, this is open for discussion, especially for recreational boating, but the economic importance of the Staande Mastroute must also be considered.

133426.explanation: BVNL supports efficient, effective, and frugal government. Investing in art is not a core municipal task.

133369.explanation: Referenda increase engagement and transparency by not only informing residents but also giving them influence over policy. This leads to better alignment between the municipal government and the community, enhancing trust in government.

133370.explanation: More room for citizen initiatives-the municipality helps, not hinders.

133371.explanation: We stand for fair taxes. Increasing the property tax (OZB) on long-term vacancies would create room to reduce the regular rate.

133372.explanation: We see no reason to reverse the previously made decision.

133373.explanation: We need agricultural land for our own facilities, and we want to maintain the appearance of the polder landscape.

133374.explanation: Subsidies for sustainable ideas from residents and entrepreneurs, provided these are not wind turbine parks or solar fields. But we should actively advocate for more subsidies from the national government.

133375.explanation: Stop the intake of extra asylum seekers in Haarlemmermeer beyond the distribution law norm.

133376.explanation: We prioritize addressing the problem of long waiting times for all residents, ensuring no group is disadvantaged.

133378.explanation: We believe the municipality must not create arbitrariness by deciding what is sustainable.

133379.explanation: We consider rooftops of large buildings a better alternative. Solar fields detract from the landscape and pose a risk to aviation around Schiphol.

133380.explanation: The municipality may financially contribute to encourage disadvantaged children to participate in sports or other social or cultural activities.

133381.explanation: There are already few monuments. Those that exist should be cherished to preserve these representatives of our small piece of history.

133382.explanation: We are generally in favor of more enforcement officers, also focused on traffic safety.

133383.explanation: We support camera surveillance in places where nuisance occurs, such as shopping areas but also unsafe locations and along dark (bike) paths.

133384.explanation: Although all forms of antisocial driving behavior must be addressed, the fatbike deserves extra attention because it is a relatively new road user that can be intimidating to others.

133385.explanation: In the past, we have already voted in favor of arming BOAs with batons.

133386.explanation: To give more local starters a financial chance in the housing market, the starter's loan should be increased, which will lead to higher municipal taxes.

133387.explanation: For the livability of the centers, it is important that residents can find suitable housing in their own environment. This supports informal care and community life, for example.

133388.explanation: The pressure from commuters and expats on our housing market is high; therefore, we believe additional space must be created for social rental housing.

133389.explanation: There must be room for development, but village centers should not be built up against each other. There must remain green spaces between them.

133390.explanation: The idea of a reciprocal requirement for welfare benefits is understandable, but it is important that the work offered truly matches the recipient’s abilities and possibilities.

133392.explanation: As far as we are concerned, this depends on the traffic situation, but in residential neighborhoods we would be in favor.

133393.explanation: International interests must be decided by the government, not by the municipality.

133394.explanation: The municipality must reduce bureaucracy. The municipality helps, not hinders. However, we want to emphasize that this should not be remedied by hiring external personnel.

133395.explanation: We have long worked to limit the number of flights; therefore, certainly no expansion of flights.

133396.explanation: We have long worked to limit the number of night flights and would prefer to see them disappear altogether.

133397.explanation: We want to reduce bureaucratic red tape in projects and applications.

133398.explanation: We believe that increasing or decreasing the property tax (OZB) should not be linked to specific policy measures.

133399.explanation: These campaigns are already provided by the national government; in our view, our municipality does not need to allocate extra funds for this.

133400.explanation: We are against expanding the number of wind turbines in the polder. We see it as a disruption to the landscape and nature, and moreover as a source of annoyance for immediate neighbors.

133401.explanation: We see no reason to allocate funding here; these are agreements between educational institutions and businesses.

133402.explanation: The contribution is already minimal, and perhaps this can be addressed with a Haarlemmermeer pass. But only if such a pass is issued and managed by our municipality itself.

133403.explanation: We see no reason to consider individual athletes as a separate population group. The facilities subsidized by the municipality are, in our view, sufficient support for a sports career.

133404.explanation: Large events often cause disturbances. However, it must remain easy to organize small local cultural events.

133406.explanation: Safety starts with prevention, cooperation, and visible enforcement. We support more police presence on the streets, as well as camera surveillance and other measures, and believe the municipality should allocate budget for this.

133407.explanation: We are in favor of more enforcement officers anyway. Without enforcement, rules become meaningless, whether this concerns parking or leaving waste near containers.

133408.explanation: We are in favor of fewer rules so that permit applications and decisions can be accelerated.

133409.explanation: We believe in smart densification, meaning more housing in existing centers and less pressure on rural areas.

133410.explanation: We believe the municipality should invest in supporting and guiding people with low incomes. Due to bureaucracy and difficulty in applying for allowances, much available support remains out of reach.

133411.explanation: We support rapid assistance for youth care and social support (WMO). Financial uncertainty leads to stress and possibly unnecessary debts.

133412.explanation: We believe in the added value of a municipal pass as long as it is managed by the municipality itself, and not linked to a commercially operated pass.

133413.explanation: Providing breakfast for children is the responsibility of the parents. The role of schools is to impart knowledge.

133414.explanation: We believe paid parking can be introduced as a targeted measure. Nearby residents and informal caregivers should be exempted through parking permits or electronic license plate registration.

133415.explanation: We believe safe cycling routes can be created without compromising other traffic or green spaces.

133416.explanation: Green areas must be preserved; therefore, when trees are cut down, replacement trees should be planted in the same area.

133417.explanation: We think programs for traffic safety are well covered by the national government and Veilig Verkeer Nederland. We would prefer more funds to be directed towards enforcing the rules.

133418.explanation: We anticipate a lot of administrative hassle here, which will ultimately lead to more frustration.

133419.explanation: We believe parking space must be preserved. Reducing parking spots in neighborhoods will lead to unnecessary frustration due to longer searches for parking by residents and visitors.

133420.explanation: The municipality should invest in informing people about, and assisting with, applying for social support (WMO).

133421.explanation: People with severe mental health problems are often not only a danger to themselves but also disrupt their immediate surroundings. Sufficient care facilities are necessary to avoid waiting lists.

133422.explanation: We recognize the importance of prevention but also see that healthcare is already under pressure. A different allocation may only be possible if the overall healthcare budget increases.

133423.explanation: We have opposed the money-wasting projects in Park 21 several times. There are plenty of other municipal projects that could use extra support.

133424.explanation: In our view, the central government already meets many of the initiatives for this community, making direct municipal support unnecessary.

133425.explanation: We see no reason or benefits for any change.

133426.explanation: We would rather see investment go to monumental trees.

133379.explanation: Agree, as long as there is no damage to nature and environment and this is not in replacement of agricultural land use.

133380.explanation: Agree, especially where the Haarlemmermeer pass might fall short.

133382.explanation: Agree, these can potentially focus on motorized traffic.

133383.explanation: Disagree, only in the context of public order disturbances according to current legislation.

133385.explanation: Agree, enforcement of the actual legal order must be ensured, and enforcement officers/investigators must be provided with the appropriate defensive equipment.

133401.explanation: Disagree, unless it concerns internship positions within municipal organizations.

133407.explanation: Agree, more expansion of the enforcement team, especially in larger centers and with area-specific officers.

133411.explanation: Disagree, the current period of 4 weeks is sufficient.

133413.explanation: Disagree, not at school, but facilitated via the home environment.

133414.explanation: Agree, any parking pressure can be regulated through blue zones and permits for residents and businesses.

133369.explanation: Residents must have influence over major decisions that affect their living environment. This strengthens engagement and trust in local democracy.

133370.explanation: Neighborhood initiatives improve livability and cohesion. A small budget helps residents implement solutions that directly improve their neighborhood.

133371.explanation: Lower taxes are attractive, but not if that leads to cuts in care and support. Residents benefit from stability and reliable services.

133372.explanation: For many elderly people, a dog is important against loneliness and for structure. It is unfair to burden only dog owners with extra costs.

133373.explanation: Agricultural land is valuable for the landscape, economy, and food security. Changes in land use must be done carefully to preserve the character of the polder.

133374.explanation: Local energy projects increase support and keep revenues within the community. This makes sustainability fairer and more affordable for residents.

133375.explanation: After a definitive rejection, return should follow. Municipalities should primarily allocate their resources to their own residents.

133376.explanation: Waiting lists have been long for a while; local residents often wait for years. Priority for status holders increases pressure on the housing market and feels unfair to many people.

133378.explanation: Sustainability is important, but a strict requirement excludes companies and harms employment. Realistic standards maintain a balance between the economy and the environment.

133379.explanation: Meadows and farmland are scarce and valuable. Roofs must be utilized first so that the landscape and living environment are not unnecessarily affected.

133380.explanation: Sport prevents health problems and social exclusion. Municipal support ensures that all children can participate.

133381.explanation: Monuments give villages identity and history. Proper maintenance prevents decay and higher costs in the future.

133382.explanation: Traffic safety is under pressure, especially for the elderly, pedestrians, and cyclists. Targeted deployment of extra enforcement officers around schools, residential areas, and busy traffic points increases safety and relieves the police. This contributes to a safe and livable municipality.

133383.explanation: Camera surveillance should not become a standard measure. Visible enforcement and neighborhood officers are more effective and better protect privacy.

133384.explanation: Reckless driving primarily endangers the elderly and children. Additional controls improve safety without new bans.

133385.explanation: Enforcement officers should mainly de-escalate and be visibly present. Arming them increases tension and primarily belongs to the police.

133386.explanation: Starter loans help both young people and the elderly with housing transitions. Stopping them would further stall the housing market.

133387.explanation: Waiting lists are long and local ties should weigh more heavily. Residents who have been waiting for years deserve priority.

133388.explanation: Affordable housing is essential, but a fixed percentage does not do justice to differences between neighborhoods and groups. 50PLUS therefore opts for customization, with sufficient social housing and life-course-proof homes for the elderly and movers, so the housing market remains balanced.

133389.explanation: Building within existing cores protects the landscape and village character. Infill and reuse are more sustainable and keep amenities close to residents.

133390.explanation: Meaningful work provides structure and self-worth. It must be appropriate and consider health and capacity.

133392.explanation: 30 km/h zones can contribute to safety, but not everywhere. For a pleasant living environment, safety, accessibility, and traffic flow must be well balanced per street and neighborhood.

133393.explanation: The municipal council is responsible for local tasks such as care, housing, and safety. International politics mainly belong to the national government, except in exceptional humanitarian situations.

133394.explanation: Good service requires sufficient staff. Fewer civil servants must not lead to longer waiting times or worse accessibility.

133395.explanation: Schiphol is important for employment, but growth must not come at the expense of health and livability. A careful balance is needed.

133396.explanation: Night flights have demonstrable effects on sleep and health. We want to limit this nuisance as much as possible and gradually phase it out, with clear limits and attention to employment and the economy.

133397.explanation: Entrepreneurs provide jobs and vibrant villages. Clear and workable rules are important; extra local rules only if they truly contribute to livability and health.

133398.explanation: Taxes must be weighed comprehensively, with attention to purchasing power and municipal priorities.

133399.explanation: Everyone must feel safe and respected in our municipality. Campaigns can contribute to more understanding, less exclusion, and a society where people continue to meet and respect each other.

133400.explanation: Wind turbines have a major impact on the living environment and health of nearby residents. 50PLUS therefore chooses energy saving and solar panels on roofs instead of additional wind turbines.

133401.explanation: Young people deserve opportunities in the labor market. Additional internships help them progress and prevent dropout.

133402.explanation: Libraries are important for meeting people, digital skills, and literacy. Free access helps our residents continue to participate.

133403.explanation: Municipal funds should go to grassroots sports and social activities. Elite athletes have other funding sources.

133404.explanation: Large events can cause nuisance and disruption. 50PLUS opts for small-scale, local activities that fit the livability of neighborhoods.

133406.explanation: Prevention and enforcement are needed to reduce crime. Quick follow-up and visibility in the neighborhood make residents safer. 50PLUS chooses to finance this by critically reviewing large projects that do not directly contribute to livability and care for residents.

133407.explanation: Enforcement officers increase visibility and enforcement in neighborhoods. They are a valuable addition to the police.

133408.explanation: Rules protect safety, quality, and the living environment. Unnecessary rules may be removed, but not everything can do without supervision.

133409.explanation: Infill and reuse keep amenities nearby and protect the landscape. Village edges should not become a standard solution.

133410.explanation: Support prevents bigger problems such as debt and care costs. Cuts increase inequality and affect vulnerable residents.

133411.explanation: Quick certainty prevents debt and stress. Young people deserve a swift and careful process.

133412.explanation: The pass helps residents participate in sports, culture, and activities. Especially elderly and low-income people benefit.

133413.explanation: No child should go to class hungry. A good breakfast helps children concentrate and participate. As temporary support, this can prevent poverty and learning delays.

133414.explanation: Free parking is unrealistic and costly. However, parking at healthcare locations must remain accessible and affordable.

133415.explanation: Safe cycling is important, but good accessibility remains essential, especially for the elderly and informal caregivers. A balanced approach for all road users works best.

133416.explanation: Green spaces are essential for health and climate adaptation. Replanting ensures the municipality remains livable and future-proof.

133417.explanation: Education and prevention reduce accidents. Young people become more aware of risks in traffic.

133418.explanation: Entrepreneurs are important for the local economy. At the same time, municipal resources should primarily be used for general facilities that are important to everyone.

133419.explanation: Parking spaces within built-up areas are essential. Older people, informal caregivers, and people with disabilities often depend on cars – both near their homes and at facilities, shops, and healthcare locations. Greenery is important, but 50PLUS believes this should not come at the expense of necessary parking spaces; greening should mainly take place elsewhere.

133420.explanation: Informal caregivers deserve recognition and support. A financial contribution helps prevent overload and makes long-term care more sustainable. They often provide care for years that would otherwise have to be professionally delivered.

133421.explanation: Appropriate care prevents nuisance and crisis situations. Additional locations provide people with peace, safety, and perspective.

133422.explanation: Prevention avoids more intensive care and improves quality of life. Especially older adults benefit from early support. 50PLUS therefore opts for greater emphasis on prevention by reprioritizing within the care budget: prevention is better and cheaper than cure.

133423.explanation: PARK21 is a large and expensive project. 50PLUS considers it irresponsible to decide to stop or continue without full clarity about agreements, costs, and social support. Therefore, we choose a careful reassessment and will proceed only if the project demonstrably contributes to livability and is financially responsible.

133424.explanation: Everyone should feel safe and accepted. Municipal support helps combat discrimination and promote inclusion.

133425.explanation: The effects on recreation, economy, and living environment are not clear-cut. 50PLUS therefore opts for a careful, neutral position.

133426.explanation: Art strengthens identity and livability in neighborhoods and villages. When resources are tight, 50PLUS prefers care and support for vulnerable residents over large-scale art projects. Small-scale, local art that contributes to the living environment is preferred.

133369.explanation: The PVV wants residents to have real say over major decisions affecting their living environment. Therefore, we advocate for a binding local referendum. The result is binding for the municipal government.

133370.explanation: Resident initiatives can contribute to livability, but municipal funds may only be used where there is demonstrable benefit, support, and feasibility. No automatic subsidies.

133371.explanation: The PVV wants lower burdens for residents and entrepreneurs. Municipalities must first cut non-core tasks before raising taxes.

133372.explanation: Dog tax is an unfair extra burden on pet owners and is disconnected from actual costs. The PVV opposes its reintroduction.

133373.explanation: Agricultural land is essential for food production and landscape. It must not be sacrificed for ideological nature or climate policies.

133374.explanation: The PVV opposes municipal subsidies for ideological climate policies. Initiatives are allowed but without coercion or waste of money.

133375.explanation: Rejected means rejected. Sheltering rejected asylum seekers is not a municipal responsibility and undermines support and provisions.

133376.explanation: Social rental housing is primarily for local residents. Priority for status holders is unjust.

133378.explanation: Ideological selection harms the economy and employment. Entrepreneurs must have the freedom to do business.

133379.explanation: Solar fields cause landscape and horizon pollution. Solar panels belong on roofs, not in open countryside.

133380.explanation: Sports promote health and social development. Children must not be excluded due to financial circumstances.

133381.explanation: Monuments deserve protection where functional and sensible, but without prestige projects or unnecessary costs.

133382.explanation: Traffic enforcement must be purposeful, but for the PVV, priority lies with public order, safety, and tackling nuisance.

133383.explanation: Camera surveillance increases safety and helps prevent crime. Residents' safety takes precedence over offenders' privacy.

133384.explanation: Extra controls improve traffic safety and reduce dangerous driving and nuisance.

133385.explanation: Boa officers must be able to protect themselves when enforcing rules in increasingly aggressive situations.

133386.explanation: Starters deserve support. The problem mainly lies with housing shortages and displacement by expats.

133387.explanation: Local residents first. That is fair and increases support for housing policies.

133388.explanation: No fixed percentages. Housing construction must align with local needs and livability.

133389.explanation: Not everything can be solved within the city. Expansion only if infrastructure and livability allow.

133390.explanation: Social assistance should not be a dead end. Social engagement is reasonable.

133392.explanation: Traffic policies must be location-specific and practical, not ideological.

133393.explanation: International politics are not a municipal task: we make no statements about international conflicts and no symbolic motions.

133394.explanation: We want to reduce the number of civil servants, but not by blind cuts. This must be done via more efficient working, less bureaucracy, and lower overhead so that services for residents remain at a good level.

133395.explanation: Schiphol is of great importance for the economy and accessibility. The municipality should not fixate on 'against growth' as a goal in itself. Growth and nuisance must be spread: Lelystad Airport is ready and fully equipped – so open it as soon as possible.

133396.explanation: Schiphol is of great importance for the economy and accessibility. The municipality should not fixate on 'against growth' as a goal in itself. Growth and nuisance must be spread: Lelystad Airport is ready and fully equipped – so open it as soon as possible.

133397.explanation: Entrepreneurs must be able to do business without extra local regulatory burdens.

133398.explanation: Property tax must not be raised at all, regardless of the purpose.

133399.explanation: No public funds for ideological awareness campaigns.

133400.explanation: No onshore wind turbines. Onshore wind turbines and solar fields in open landscapes cause horizon pollution and damage nature and recreation. Therefore, we oppose them.

133401.explanation: More municipal funding does not automatically lead to more internships and is unrelated to job opportunities. Moreover, the labor market for practically trained youth is currently good. Internships are primarily the responsibility of education and employers.

133402.explanation: Accessibility is important, but free access for everyone is not a goal in itself.

133403.explanation: Municipal funds are intended for amateur sports and associations.

133404.explanation: Events are welcome but only within clear boundaries: safety, livability, and costs must remain manageable. In case of structural nuisance, permits will be tightened or not renewed.

133406.explanation: Safety is an absolute core task of the municipality.

133407.explanation: Visible enforcement increases safety and trust in the neighborhood.

133408.explanation: Fewer rules accelerate housing construction and reduce costs.

133409.explanation: Primarily more building, depending on location, support, and infrastructure.

133410.explanation: Poverty and minimum income policies require targeted customization: help for those who really need it, without additional burdens on all residents.

133412.explanation: The Haarlemmermeer pass is too broad a tool; there are other safety nets that can be used more purposefully.

133413.explanation: Parents remain primarily responsible; targeted help where needed.

133414.explanation: Making parking free will cause nuisance. The current parking policy is adequate. Where this is not the case, practical customization should be provided.

133415.explanation: No ideological redistribution of public space.

133416.explanation: Green is important, but no rigid rules.

133417.explanation: Prevention and education increase traffic safety.

133419.explanation: Parking spaces are necessary for accessibility.

133420.explanation: Support yes, but caregiving should not become a compulsory business model.

133421.explanation: Vulnerable people should not fall between the cracks.

133422.explanation: Care funds should primarily go to direct care.

133423.explanation: The costs and social benefits are no longer justifiable.

133424.explanation: No public funds for identity and activism policies.

133425.explanation: Change only if safety and the economy are harmed.

133426.explanation: No public funds for elitist or ideological art.

133369.explanation: Residents must have real influence on important decisions. Referendums can be an important instrument for this, provided it is clear what qualifies as an 'important decision' and there remains room for customization.

133370.explanation: By supporting residents with good plans, we strengthen influence and involvement in the neighborhood. The municipality should enable this through transparent and accessible neighborhood budgets.

133371.explanation: A reduction in property tax (OZB) may be necessary to limit housing costs. Cuts should not automatically come at the expense of vulnerable facilities but require clear political choices.

133372.explanation: The dog tax can contribute to a clean and safe public space. The proceeds must demonstrably be used for facilities such as dog walking areas and enforcement, so dog owners also directly benefit.

133373.explanation: Nature and agriculture do not have to be opposites. Protecting nature is necessary for health, biodiversity, and climate. However, this must happen in balance, with respect for farmers and their livelihood security.

133374.explanation: The energy transition will only succeed if residents can participate in decisions and benefits. By putting energy projects as much as possible in the hands of residents, we strengthen support, keep revenues local, and ensure a fair distribution of advantages and burdens.

133375.explanation: No one should end up on the street. Humane shelter prevents nuisance and insecurity and provides peace and clarity. The municipality must take responsibility here, together with the national government.

133376.explanation: Refugees with a residence permit have the right to a fresh start. At the same time, the municipality must continue to invest in sufficient affordable housing so that this does not come at the expense of other home seekers.

133378.explanation: The municipality must focus on a future-proof economy. By attracting sustainable companies, we protect the environment, health, and create fair jobs for residents.

133379.explanation: The energy transition requires space, but it must be used carefully. Solar panels on meadows can be an option if other locations are insufficient and if this is done with consideration for landscape, agriculture, nature, and traffic. Roofs and business parks have priority.

133380.explanation: Sport is essential for health, self-confidence, and social development. No child may be excluded because of lack of money. The municipality must guarantee equal opportunities.

133381.explanation: Monuments tell the story of our society. Cuts here mean loss of cultural heritage and identity. Protection is an investment in the future and tourism.

133382.explanation: Traffic safety requires visible enforcement. Additional community officers ensure safer streets, protect vulnerable road users, and enhance livability in neighborhoods.

133383.explanation: We want more camera surveillance where necessary-but not at the expense of privacy.

133384.explanation: Dangerous behavior on fatbikes leads to insecurity. Targeted controls protect pedestrians and other road users and establish clear rules for everyone.

133385.explanation: BOAs must be able to perform their work safely. A baton can be necessary in threatening situations, provided it is used by well-trained personnel and applied proportionally.

133386.explanation: First-time buyers face challenges in the housing market. The starter loan helps young people and families secure their first home and promotes equal opportunities.

133387.explanation: Residents should not have to wait for years outside their own municipality. Priority strengthens ties with the municipality and ensures stable neighborhoods.

133388.explanation: The housing crisis requires decisive choices. More social rental housing is needed to keep living affordable for people with low and middle incomes.

133389.explanation: The housing shortage calls for space. With careful planning, expansion outside villages can contribute to affordable housing without compromising livability.

133390.explanation: Participation in society is important but must never become a punishment or obligation. The municipality should support and activate people on welfare with tailored measures, taking health, caregiving, and personal circumstances into account.

133392.explanation: Traffic safety is paramount, especially around schools and residential areas. A general 30 km/h rule is not appropriate everywhere. The municipality should assess each street for what is safe and logical, together with residents.

133393.explanation: Municipalities may speak out against injustice and human rights violations. Solidarity and humanity do not stop at national borders.

133394.explanation: Good service requires sufficient and skilled civil servants. The problem lies not in the number of people but in bureaucracy and inefficiency. We must invest in better services, not in cuts.

133395.explanation: The municipality should not automatically oppose growth but focus on protecting health, living environment, and employment. Growth must never come at the expense of residents.

133396.explanation: Night flights cause serious health complaints. The peace and sleep of residents must weigh more heavily than economic interests.

133397.explanation: Entrepreneurs need clarity and space. Additional local regulations hinder initiative and harm the local economy.

133398.explanation: If taxes rise, it must be clearly for the benefit of residents. Investments in sustainability reduce costs in the long term and improve quality of life.
