133399.explanation: Discrimination undermines trust and opportunities. Investing in awareness and equality is necessary for a just society.

133400.explanation: Sustainability is important, but more wind turbines are not desirable everywhere. Location, support, and impact on the living environment must be guiding factors.

133401.explanation: Practically trained young people deserve opportunities. More internships prevent dropouts and strengthen the link between education and work.

133402.explanation: Libraries are essential for language, development, and social interaction. Free access increases opportunities for everyone, regardless of income.

133403.explanation: Professional sports contribute to health, serve as role models, and foster social connection. Subsidies may be justified when there is demonstrable social added value.

133404.explanation: Events bring liveliness and economic impulses. However, nuisance must be limited and support from local residents must be central.

133406.explanation: Safety is a basic right. We want to invest more in prevention, youth work, and enforcement. This prevents bigger problems later.

133407.explanation: Additional enforcement officers provide visibility, approachability, and livable neighborhoods. Prevention and proximity come first.

133408.explanation: Unnecessary regulations slow down housing construction. Less bureaucracy means faster affordable homes while maintaining quality.

133409.explanation: Building on village edges offers room for growth without overburdening village centers, provided greenery and facilities are included.

133410.explanation: The focus must be on structural solutions such as work, education, and affordable housing. Support remains necessary but should empower people rather than create dependence.

133411.explanation: Rapid support prevents debt and hopelessness. Young people have the right to certainty and prospects.

133412.explanation: The pass increases accessibility to sports, culture, and facilities for people with low incomes and promotes inclusion.

133413.explanation: No child should go hungry to school. A good breakfast improves health, concentration, and learning outcomes.

133414.explanation: Parking must remain affordable and fair. Free parking sounds attractive but costs the municipality a lot of money. We want tailored solutions: affordable parking for residents and visitors without harming livability.

133415.explanation: Promoting cycling is good but must not come at the expense of accessibility. The municipality must seek smart solutions for all modes of transport.

133416.explanation: Green spaces are essential for climate, health, and livability. Replanting compensates for loss and strengthens biodiversity.

133417.explanation: Education prevents accidents. Raising youth awareness of risks saves lives.

133418.explanation: Local entrepreneurs deserve support. By compensating businesses for parking costs on shopping evenings, we stimulate the local economy and ensure lively shopping areas.

133419.explanation: Greenery is important but not at the expense of accessibility. There must be a balance between livability and practical needs.

133420.explanation: Informal caregiving is indispensable. Financial recognition prevents overload and acknowledges their commitment.

133421.explanation: Good care prevents escalation and nuisance. Investing in care is investing in safety and human dignity.

133422.explanation: Prevention is better than cure. Prevention saves costs and prevents human suffering.

133423.explanation: PARK21 offers opportunities for recreation, work, and meeting. Development must continue with attention to the environment and participation.

133424.explanation: DENK believes every community in Haarlemmermeer should be helped by the municipality to address their specific interests and challenges. However, DENK finds it important that every community is assisted simultaneously.

133425.explanation: Decision-making requires careful balancing of safety, economy, and living environment. No black-and-white choices, but tailored solutions based on facts.

133426.explanation: In times of scarcity, basic services must take priority. Art is valuable but must not displace essential tasks.

133369.explanation: A referendum is an option, not an obligation. We have a referendum ordinance in Haarlemmermeer. So the possibility exists.

133370.explanation: For a well-founded plan, it is possible to allocate financial resources. Improving the neighborhood together means taking responsibility together and looking after it. However, it is not mandatory to spend money on this.

133371.explanation: The property tax (OZB) contributes to maintenance and improvement of local facilities such as parks and community centers, and the financing of public education, emergency services, and other essential programs that strengthen the community.

133372.explanation: It was only recently abolished, reintroducing it is now unnecessary.

133373.explanation: The ambition to convert agricultural land into nature reserves is an important part of Dutch nature and nitrogen policy, with the main goal of expanding the Dutch Nature Network (NNN) and achieving environmental targets.

133374.explanation: Financial support can help but must always be carefully considered per situation. For sustainable ideas, subsidies are often available from the province or the national government. Therefore, a case-by-case approach is necessary.

133375.explanation: We support shelter for asylum seekers during the procedure. Once an asylum application is rejected, the responsibility, according to Hart voor Haarlemmermeer, lies with the national government: for temporary shelter and supervision of return according to policy, not with the municipality.

133376.explanation: Everyone should have a fair chance to get a rental home, regardless of origin. Of course, urgent cases will still receive attention so that those who really need help get it quickly.

133378.explanation: Not every company can immediately operate fully sustainably, especially start-ups or companies in transition. It is more effective to help them become sustainable through policy, advice, and subsidies than to exclude them completely.

133379.explanation: The municipality currently generates sufficient energy with existing solar panels on meadows. Small-scale placement of solar panels by farmers on their own land for their own use remains possible.

133380.explanation: Through the scheme 'Haarlemmermeer does participate!' and also the Youth Fund Sport & Culture, which pays contributions and materials. In addition, subsidies are available for sports clubs. So it is possible.

133381.explanation: We must preserve the municipality’s heritage in a sustainable way for the future.

133382.explanation: And not only for traffic safety. Think also of enforcing youth nuisance, checking dumped waste, or other matters.

133383.explanation: To prevent nuisance, it is unfortunately necessary to better protect shopping areas. Although this conflicts with our privacy feelings, safety takes precedence here.

133384.explanation: Police and enforcement officers should point out behavior issues to fatbikers, provided there is reason to do so.

133385.explanation: Only after thorough training and of course only in certain situations.

133386.explanation: The starter loan often makes it possible for first-time buyers to purchase a home.

133387.explanation: We believe that people from our own municipality should be first in line for a social rental home. It is important you can continue living in the neighborhood where you grew up or where you work.

133388.explanation: Hart van Haarlemmermeer would like to see this up to 50%. And we believe that this is possible.

133389.explanation: We see opportunities in expanding small villages, for example on the edges of towns. More housing is especially needed in these small villages.

133390.explanation: Where possible, social engagement is requested from people receiving social assistance benefits.

133392.explanation: In our small villages, a 30 km/h speed limit could be introduced, but in Hoofddorp or Nieuw-Vennep this may be more difficult for traffic flow.

133393.explanation: Although the municipality does not conduct foreign policy, the council can take a moral or symbolic stance that sends a signal to the national government and the international community.

133394.explanation: We have a severe shortage of civil servants to carry out all our tasks.

133395.explanation: With the growth of Haarlemmermeer and the economy, Schiphol will want to adapt as well. In cooperation with Schiphol, we will focus on clear agreements. Growth must not come at the expense of our residents’ health.

133396.explanation: A good night’s rest is important for our residents.

133397.explanation: Sometimes municipal rules are simply necessary. Think of waste collection, the environment, or the sale of prohibited substances.

133398.explanation: The property tax (OZB) may only increase for various investments if that would be necessary.

133399.explanation: As long as necessary, we follow national government campaigns in this area, but if needed, the municipality can also get involved itself.

133400.explanation: An agreement is an agreement. We would prefer even fewer wind turbines; the solar panel fields will soon already provide enough energy.

133401.explanation: Our growing municipality urgently needs skilled workers: we invest in practice-oriented education and ensure that companies offer attractive internships. We desperately need skilled workers!

133402.explanation: The core library service is already free for all residents, but borrowing (especially e-books) and extended subscriptions naturally cost money, except for youth under 18.

133403.explanation: Public subsidies, in our view, are not intended for individual professional athletes, but for amateur sports and community sports facilities.

133404.explanation: It is important to maintain a good balance with the living environment and to pay attention to possible nuisance such as noise. Visitors will also make a positive contribution to the local economy through spending on hotels, restaurants, transport, and shops.

133406.explanation: We deploy financial resources against crime specifically on prevention, visible enforcement, and good cooperation between the municipality, police, and social partners. Attention to youth, vulnerable neighborhoods, and undermining crime is essential.

133407.explanation: More enforcement officers provide a safer, cleaner, and more livable municipality while simultaneously relieving the police and staying closer to residents.

133408.explanation: Additional building rules have simply been agreed upon in Haarlemmermeer. Think of height limits and Schiphol, or the view corridor along the Ringdijk.

133409.explanation: By looking more broadly and creatively at possible building locations, local housing needs can be better met without forcing residents to leave their village.

133410.explanation: The municipality has an important responsibility to support low-income residents, combat poverty, and promote equal opportunities. This support prevents larger social and societal problems.

133411.explanation: What is the added value? One can examine per case whether urgency is needed, and then facilitate that. And why only for youth?

133412.explanation: The Haarlemmermeer pass cannot always accommodate everyone's situation. We advocate for more tailored individual arrangements so that support better matches individual needs.

133413.explanation: We agree if it turns out that children do not get breakfast at home. But primary responsibility lies with the parents. However, a child should never be hungry in the classroom.

133414.explanation: The municipality uses this income for the management and maintenance of parking spaces and traffic facilities.

133415.explanation: In our view, this should not come at the expense of space for cars. For example, if parking spots need to be removed, they can be relocated within the same neighborhood.

133416.explanation: The current rule is to replant one tree for every tree that is cut down. This arrangement is working well.

133417.explanation: Building a traffic safety plan together with young people in which they can move confidently, safely, and feeling involved.

133418.explanation: Parking spaces are part of the normal operations of entrepreneurs. Instead of compensating them, the municipality should better invest in collective solutions such as public transport or central parking facilities.

133419.explanation: As long as the parking space can be restored elsewhere, it should be allowed.

133420.explanation: Informal caregivers need support, which goes beyond financial compensation. Think of replacement during illness or having a sympathetic ear to share their story.

133421.explanation: We are responsible for good support and participation of people who otherwise fall through the cracks due to their psychological problems.

133422.explanation: At this moment, we see no necessity to allocate extra resources. Should the situation require it in the future, we are willing to reassess and adjust if necessary.

133423.explanation: A lot of money has already been invested. Let's finish this project now, then at least the money will not have been wasted.

133424.explanation: Municipalities have the task to support all residents, especially vulnerable groups. LGBTQIA+ policy often requires local tailoring: what is needed in one municipality varies by context.

133425.explanation: The fixed Mastroute partly runs over the ring canal. The province manages this part. We see no reason to divert it.

133426.explanation: Art in public spaces makes neighborhoods more attractive and gives a city or village its own character. It fosters recognition, pride, and connection among residents.

statements.moreInfo: At the moment there are no municipal cameras in these shopping areas.

statements.title: Of the newly built homes in Haarlemmermeer, at least 40% should be social rental housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social rental housing.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.moreInfo: Social rental homes are homes with a rent of up to €932.93 per month. These homes are intended for residents with lower incomes. Single people who want to rent a house on their own qualify for social housing if they earn less than €51,537 gross per year. For households of two or more people this limit is €56,910. At the moment 22% of all homes in Haarlemmermeer are social rental housing.

statements.title: For important decisions, the municipality should ask residents for their opinion via a referendum.

titleParts.text: Bij belangrijke besluiten moet de gemeente de inwoners om hun mening vragen via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.moreInfo: In a referendum a government poses a single-issue question to voters. They can vote for or against. In a national referendum all Dutch citizens aged 18 or older may vote. In a municipal referendum all residents of that municipality aged 18 or older may vote. In the Netherlands the result of a referendum is not binding, which means that a government does not have to follow the result and can make a different decision. At the moment it is possible to organize an advisory referendum in the municipality of Haarlemmermeer.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.moreInfo: Everyone who owns a house or a commercial property pays tax to the municipality for it. That tax is called property tax (OZB). The amount of the OZB is a percentage of the estimated value of the property, the WOZ value. The municipality makes this estimate every year. At the moment the OZB for homeowners in Haarlemmermeer is 0.06487% of the WOZ value. People who own a home but do not live in it, or who live in a house that is not theirs, pay more.

statements.moreInfo: For young people a period of four weeks currently applies. A municipality can choose not to impose this period when it concerns youths who are in difficult circumstances.

statements.moreInfo: It can happen that a municipality wants to impose rules that are not already set by the national government, for example in the area of sustainability.

shootoutStatements.moreInfo: De gemeenteraad heeft een gebied aangewezen waar mogelijk windmolens kunnen komen. Omdat de gemeente zelf geen beslissing mag nemen over het plaatsen van windmolens, heeft de gemeente Haarlemmermeer met de provincie afgesproken dat onderzocht wordt of deze locatie geschikt is voor windmolens. Ook kan de gemeente de provincie aangeven wat haar wensen zijn. Onderzoek heeft uitgewezen dat er ruimte zou zijn voor vier tot elf windmolens. Verder onderzoek moet gaan bepalen of de windmolens er ook echt gaan komen en hoeveel dat er zijn. Als de gemeente Haarlemmermeer daarna nog meer windmolens in de gemeente wil, dan zal het dus met de provincie in gesprek moeten. De uiteindelijke beslissing ligt bij de provincie.
